options.label: Fatebound (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one the following benefits.

Dream Gift. A god, Norn, or other powerful being has given you a gift in a dream. When you awoke, you had a Common magic item of the GM’s choice that may aid you.

Fate’s Blessing. Once per day, while you are Bloodied, you can use your Reaction to get Advantage on a saving throw.

Your fate is written. Make it glorious.

Many warriors loudly boast of their desire for a noble death or an honorable end in battle, to thus earn a seat in the great hall of Valhalla. These boasts are but hopeful wishes since most individuals never see the path of their future. You, however, are not most individuals. You have been blessed, or perhaps cursed, with the knowledge of your end, though this knowledge doesn’t tell you if you succeed or fail in the circumstance surrounding your death. You might know you will die facing a dark warlord, but you don’t know if you will defeat her. Despite this, the Norns have given you a rare opportunity. The knowledge you possess will help you ensure your death is worthy of the sagas.

Some of those who are Fatebound are marked from birth. Others learn of their fate from seers, shamans, or divine visions. Some heroes earn this thread after engaging in activities that move the gods or the Norns to reward them with such a potent end.

Fatebound and Death. Keep in mind that having this thread may mean you can’t die before your fated time, but it doesn’t mean you will reach that point whole and healthy. While your fate is inevitable, luck alone determines your condition when you meet that end. Do you lead a glorious charge across the battlefield? Or are you left guarding the baggage? Has every confrontation leading up to your end prepared you to defeat your ultimate foe? Or has a lifetime of avoidance left you ill-prepared?

If a Fatebound PC is killed before their time, and magical resurrection is not an option, a GM could instead choose for them to be injured in a significant way—such as the loss of a limb or eye. These injuries should not be able to be healed without high-level magic. Alternatively, perhaps they lose one of their powerful magical items instead of their lives, or they might suffer a level of Exhaustion until they reach their next milestone. The GM and player should agree on this complication.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Fatebound Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

GM Note: The Fatebound is an unusual thread in that its final milestone specifically describes the manner in which a character might meet with a permanent death, likely as part of the final accomplishment of their goal, and then empowers the PCs’ allies as a reward. Most importantly, this thread should be something the player consciously selects.

Fatebound Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will die only after finding and facing the mightiest dragon in the land.
2 I will be slain by Utgard-Loki while attempting to thwart his plan to slay the gods.
3 To defend my people from invasion, I pledge to pay any price, including my own life.
4 I shall search for and find a fabled lost treasure, but I will die in the process.
5 I will rise to become the North’s greatest reaver, then perish battling the World Serpent.
6 I seek the truth behind the fall of Nordheim, though it will cost my life to learn it.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Strength of Wyrd. While you are Bloodied, once per turn you may add your Proficiency Bonus to a damage roll. You may use this damage bonus a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Enduring Wyrd. When you take damage, you may use your Reaction to gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You cannot use this feature again until you complete a Short or Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain your choice of one the following benefits.

Dream Gift. A god, Norn, or other powerful being has given you a gift in a dream. When you awoke, you had a Common magic item of the GM’s choice that may aid you.

Fate’s Blessing. Once per day, while you are Bloodied, you can use your Reaction to get Advantage on a saving throw.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 11)
options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Doom Delayed. When you would be killed, you may instead declare yourself as Stable with 0 Hit Points. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Knight of Fate. You have become a legend in a territory, and the monarch there has knighted you, granting you holdings that will revert back to the monarchy upon your death. You have Advantage on Charisma-based checks with the people of this land and are frequently showered with minor gifts and favors there.

Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

options.label: Fatebound (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefits.

Last Act of Fate. At a moment you have predetermined to be the time when you are to die, when you are reduced 0 Hit Points or killed outright, you may (no action required) instead set your Hit Points to 1. You lose any detrimental conditions, and any harmful effects on you are ended immediately. Until the end of your next turn, you gain Advantage on all attack rolls and ability checks, you have Immunity to all damage and conditions (and other effects at the GM’s discretion), and you add your character level to all damage rolls.

At the end of your next turn, you die and can never be restored to life.

Glorious End. If you achieve your destiny in a way that earns you a seat in Valhalla (or some other appropriate afterlife), the allies who witness your death have Advantage on all d20 rolls for 24 hours as they are inspired by your heroism.

options.label: Herald (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefits.

Storyteller’s Knack. You learn three languages of your choice.

Performer’s Reward. If you have at least 3 days of downtime between adventures, you can earn GP equal to 10 times your character level by performing your stories in taverns, on the street, or for powerful patrons. Additional downtime events don’t increase this reward.

Your stories don’t just recount events . . . they shape history.

Storytellers are revered in the Northlands. Whether a skald sharing tales around a fire, a lore keeper gathering sagas to pass down the generations, or a grizzled reaver frightening young warriors by recalling harrowing battles, the tradition of collecting stories is respected across nearly all cultures in the region. As a Herald, you are a proud, if temporary, part of this tradition, driven by a need to spread stories during your travels to accomplish a specific end.

Perhaps your stories encourage downtrodden karls to rise up against their cruel jarl, warn a region about an approaching invasion, or convince warriors to join your cause. Part skald, part herald, part envoy, you will use your tales and stories to sway the minds of your audience and help shape the world.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Herald Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

GM Note. This thread brings flavorful Northlands themes into a campaign and can be taken up by characters of any class. However, it can tread on the toes of any Bards in the party. Consult with your Bard players before allowing a character to take on this thread to make sure no feelings will be hurt.

Herald Goals
d6 Goal
1 The feared invasion from Jotunheim approaches, and the country must be warned.
2 By telling the sagas, I will gather support for the greatest raid the North has ever launched.
3 My tales flatter Thor and Wotan in the hope they will support our struggle against the forces of Chernobog and Angrboda.
4 I spread the word of the great crusade to eradicate the darkness beneath Tanserhall. Heed my call!
5 I seek a lost treasure of Hyperborea, and by spreading tales of similar treasures, I hope to find a path to a hidden vault.
6 To rouse the people against their evil king, I shall assemble an array of sorrowful tales of corruption that cannot be ignored.

options.label: Herald (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Enthralling Speaker. You gain proficiency in the Performance skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Expertise. Additionally, while performing before an audience, you can cast either Charm Person or Suggestion as part of your performance without requiring somatic or material components. Use Charisma as your spellcasting ability score. After casting one of these spells using this feature, you can’t use the feature again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.description:

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Persuasive Words. If you make a Charisma check to change a creature's attitude or convince them of your cause, you may treat a roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10. You can do this a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier per Long Rest.

options.label: Herald (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain one of the following benefits.

Heroic Saga. Your tales grow so powerful that they can briefly tamper with luck. After you spend at least 10 minutes telling an inspiring story or legend, choose either one creature who can hear you, or up to three creatures. You can’t select yourself as a target. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until for 7 days.

Depending on the number of targets, creatures can receive one of the following.

Luck Boon (Three Targets). Once before the next Long Rest, a target can expend their boon to gain Advantage on a saving throw, attack roll, or ability check.

Wyrd Boon (One Target). Once before their next Long Rest, if the target does not succeed on a saving throw or attack roll, they can choose to succeed instead. An attack roll succeeding in this way results in the minimum needed to succeed (not a Critical Hit, for example).

Song of Matrimony. A member of the royal family in a particular land has fallen in love with you and your performances, and the feeling is mutual. You are engaged to marry into the royal family.

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefit.

Predator’s Mark. You have Advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks made to track creatures, and on a failed check it only takes you half the time to reacquire the tracks (5 minutes in a confined area, or half an hour outdoors).

Neither storm nor night nor bloody fang shall slow your relentless pursuit.

Hunting is as old as the Northlands itself. From volcanic peaks to frigid fens and aurora-bathed glaciers, the tales of hunters are sung alongside those of heroes and gods. While some may hunt for survival or vengeance, you are different. You are a hunter to be feared, as you hunt for the thrill of the chase and the glory of victory.

As a Legend Hunter, you may have been chosen by the Norns, blessed by a god, or forged in trials of blood and grift. No matter how you come to it, your extraordinary search for greater and greater prey sets you apart from those who hunt merely for survival. You do not merely forge trails; you craft sagas. You do not merely slay beasts; you end legends to become a legend.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Legend Hunter Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

Legend Hunter Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will prove my valor by tracking down and slaying a legendary monster that has been destroying villages.
2 I hunt for a mythical two-headed jotun somewhere in the Bleak Expanse.
3 Besting a great dragon in its lair shall be my ultimate victory.
4 I will go beneath the waves to bring low the mother of all krakens.
5 I will bring back the head of Fenris.
6 My legend will be assured when I go among the roots of Yggdrasil to slay Nidhogg.

options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Unclouded Sight. Your honed senses let you ignore obscurement caused by weather, such as rain, snow, or fog. This includes magical clouds and weather effects, such as Fog Cloud and Sleet Storm, but not Darkness.

Reliable Senses. After failing an Intelligence (Investigation), Wisdom (Perception), or Wisdom (Survival) check, you can reroll the check and take the higher result. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Least Milestone Feature

options.description:

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Finish the Fight. When you cause a creature to become Bloodied, your next attack that hits it becomes a Critical Hit. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

options.label: Legend Hunter (Level 17)
options.description:
Superior Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Legend Breaker. When you hit a creature that has Legendary Resistance or Legendary Actions, you can choose to forgo dealing damage to force that creature to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength or Wisdom modifier and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the target becomes unable use its Legendary Resistance or its Legendary Actions. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

The Ultimate Favor. You performed a service for a powerful healer, who was caught in an animal trap set by another hunter. In return, they have offered to cast a single spell of healing of 6th to 9th level on you or a creature of your choice, at a time of your choosing, even if this puts them at great risk. This should be a spell that heals, regenerates, resurrects, or that removes conditions or curses.

Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Finish the Fight. When you cause a creature to become Bloodied, your next attack that hits it becomes a Critical Hit. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.label: Sworn Huskarl (Level 1)
options.description:
Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefit.

Jarl’s Authority. The authority granted you by your loyal service can sway the opinions of others. Before rolling an Intimidation or Persuasion check to influence someone who knows and respects (or fears) your ruler, you can choose to treat the d20 roll as if you’d rolled a natural 15. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

You carry out the will of your king, no matter the risk.

You have pledged your loyalty to an important personage, promising to guard them or travel across the Northlands in their service, risking danger as you seek to accomplish a great task. In the past, it was expected that a huskarl would swear to serve a lord for life or until they were dismissed. While jarls still take huskarls in that way, they also accept the service of huskarls on a temporary basis, to perform specific deeds or until certain threats are dealt with. These are the types of huskarls described in this thread.

The sworn huskarl enjoys a position of respect within their lord’s retinue. More than a mere bodyguard, you are a trusted agent acting on your ruler’s behalf. You carry some of their authority with you, but that also comes with the responsibility to act in ways they would approve. Sworn huskarls who bring shame to their jarl don’t remain in service for long.

A jarl takes on a new huskarl with a simple ceremony in which the huskarl swears an oath in the longhouse before a gathering of other members of the felag. During the oath-swearing, the goals or timeframe for the service are mentioned (unless the jarl keeps your mission a secret). Traditionally, your reasons for serving are respect for the lord, payment, or power. However, in some cases your oath may hold a secret. Perhaps you made it under threat of violence or under false pretenses. Or maybe your lord doesn’t deserve such loyalty. You might serve under protest or with your own goal that’s unknown to your ruler.

Goals. Work with your GM to create a goal specific to your campaign or use one from the Sworn Huskarl Goals table. The specific nature of each milestone is dependent on the goal selected and should be agreed upon by both player and GM. Use the suggested milestone benefits described in this section or create new ones based on the rules for creating a thread.

Sworn Huskarl Goals
d6 Goal
1 I will find my king’s long-lost child so that my kingdom will have a true heir.
2 My queen depends on me to find and slay the pretender who claims a blood right to her throne.
3 I must root out a hidden sect of the Cult of Ragnarok that grows within my jarl’s land.
4 Giant raiding parties threaten the land. My jarl calls upon me to destroy them and make the land safe once more.
5 I can only earn my freedom by finding the stolen crown of my sadistic monarch.
6 I serve only until I can discover a way to overthrow this corrupt king, who took the throne through dark sorcery.

options.label: Sworn Huskarl (Level 5)
options.description:
Lesser Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain your choice of one of the following benefits.

Extreme Loyalty. Your dedication to your ruler protects you from the control of others.

If you end your turn with the Charmed condition, you may choose to take Psychic damage equal to your character level, then end that condition. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Jarl’s Gift. To help you carry out their will, your ruler gives you your choice of one of the following uncommon magic items (or another you and your GM agree upon): Bracers of Archery, Defender’s Honey, Sentinel Shield, +1 Wand of the War Mage, or +1 Weapon.

Least Milestone Feature

When you achieve the first milestone of this thread, you gain the following benefit.

Jarl’s Authority. The authority granted you by your loyal service can sway the opinions of others. Before rolling an Intimidation or Persuasion check to influence someone who knows and respects (or fears) your ruler, you can choose to treat the d20 roll as if you’d rolled a natural 15. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

options.label: Sworn Huskarl (Level 11)
options.description:
Greater Milestone Feature

When you achieve this level of milestone, you gain the following benefit.

Undying Loyalty. The strength of your oath steels you when the tides of battle turn against you. When you are reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can use this feature to instead set your Hit Points to be equal to your character level. This doesn’t help you against an effect that would kill you instantly without dealing damage. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again for 7 days.

You must use one for yourself and apply the other to a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. You must use both rolls before the start of your next turn or they are wasted. You can sketch perto as often as you want, provided you remain within 100 feet of Loki.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch perto in the air (no action required). Upon doing do, you gain a d6, called a perto die, that you can roll, adding the result to a single d20 roll you make before the end of your next turn.

Lesser Power: When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes a d20 roll, you can use a Reaction to sketch perto. If you do so, roll your perto die. The creature’s roll increases or decreases (your choice) by the result of your roll.

Greater Power: Your perto die increases to a d8. Additionally, after you sketch perto and add the result of your d8 roll to a d20 roll, you can hide immediately (no action required), providing the terrain and circumstances permit it.

options.label: Sowilo (Level 1)
options.description: Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch sowilo in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, you shed Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet until the end of your next turn. While surrounded by this radiance, you have Advantage on Charisma checks and saves.

options.label: Sowilo (Level 2)
options.description:
Lesser Power: When you sketch sowilo, you and a friendly creature within the radius of your light enjoy resistance to Necrotic damage. Additionally, Aberrations and Undead entering the radius of your light, or starting their turn within it, take Radiant damage equal to your level.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch sowilo in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, you shed Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet until the end of your next turn. While surrounded by this radiance, you have Advantage on Charisma checks and saves.

options.label: Sowilo (Level 7)
options.description:
Greater Power: When you sketch sowilo, you can use a Bonus Action to inspire a Friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The creature can use its Reaction immediately to move up to half its Speed and make one melee attack.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch sowilo in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, you shed Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet until the end of your next turn. While surrounded by this radiance, you have Advantage on Charisma checks and saves.

Lesser Power: When you sketch sowilo, you and a friendly creature within the radius of your light enjoy resistance to Necrotic damage. Additionally, Aberrations and Undead entering the radius of your light, or starting their turn within it, take Radiant damage equal to your level.

options.label: Sowilo (Level 12)
options.description:
Patron Power: While within 100 feet of Baldur, when you sketch sowilo, you can use an action to focus your radiance into a beam of pure light. Make a ranged spell attack against a target using your Dexterity or Charisma modifier. On a hit, the target takes 5d8 Radiant damage and has the Blinded condition until the start of your next turn. This immediately ends your rune and its aura of light. You can sketch sowilo and use it this way as often as you want, provided you remain within 100 feet of Baldur.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch sowilo in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, you shed Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 20 feet until the end of your next turn. While surrounded by this radiance, you have Advantage on Charisma checks and saves.

Lesser Power: When you sketch sowilo, you and a friendly creature within the radius of your light enjoy resistance to Necrotic damage. Additionally, Aberrations and Undead entering the radius of your light, or starting their turn within it, take Radiant damage equal to your level.

Greater Power: When you sketch sowilo, you can use a Bonus Action to inspire a Friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The creature can use its Reaction immediately to move up to half its Speed and make one melee attack.

options.label: Thurisaz (Level 1)
options.description: Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch thurisaz in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, your melee weapon attacks deal 1d4 Lightning damage until the end of your next turn. Additionally, if you deal this extra Lightning damage to a target, you can cause a clap of thunder to push the target up to 10 feet away from you.

options.label: Thurisaz (Level 2)
options.description:
Lesser Power: When you sketch thurisaz, the extra damage increases to 1d6. In addition, if you strike a creature with a melee attack and deal the extra Lightning damage, a second creature you can see within 5 feet of your initial target also takes the extra Lightning damage.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch thurisaz in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, your melee weapon attacks deal 1d4 Lightning damage until the end of your next turn. Additionally, if you deal this extra Lightning damage to a target, you can cause a clap of thunder to push the target up to 10 feet away from you.

options.label: Thurisaz (Level 7)
options.description:
Greater Power: When you sketch thurisaz, the extra damage increases to 1d8. In addition, any melee weapon you wield gains the Thrown property with a range of 20/60 ft. If you strike a creature with a Thrown weapon, it magically reappears in your hand at the end of your turn.

Lesser Power: When you sketch thurisaz, the extra damage increases to 1d6. In addition, if you strike a creature with a melee attack and deal the extra Lightning damage, a second creature you can see within 5 feet of your initial target also takes the extra Lightning damage.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch thurisaz in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, your melee weapon attacks deal 1d4 Lightning damage until the end of your next turn. Additionally, if you deal this extra Lightning damage to a target, you can cause a clap of thunder to push the target up to 10 feet away from you.

options.label: Thurisaz (Level 12)
options.description:
Patron Power: While within 100 feet of Thor, when you sketch thurisaz, you can use an action to strike the ground with a weapon, causing lightning to arc from the impact to strike up to three targets you can see within 60 feet of you. Make a ranged spell attack using your Strength or Dexterity modifier. On a hit, a target takes 3d12 Lightning damage. Then the rune ends. You can sketch and use thurisaz this way as often as you want, provided you remain within 100 feet of Thor.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch thurisaz in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, your melee weapon attacks deal 1d4 Lightning damage until the end of your next turn. Additionally, if you deal this extra Lightning damage to a target, you can cause a clap of thunder to push the target up to 10 feet away from you.

Greater Power: When you sketch thurisaz, the extra damage increases to 1d8. In addition, any melee weapon you wield gains the Thrown property with a range of 20/60 ft. If you strike a creature with a Thrown weapon, it magically reappears in your hand at the end of your turn.

Lesser Power: When you sketch thurisaz, the extra damage increases to 1d6. In addition, if you strike a creature with a melee attack and deal the extra Lightning damage, a second creature you can see within 5 feet of your initial target also takes the extra Lightning damage.

options.label: Tiwaz (Level 1)
options.description: Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch tiwaz in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you to become your focused target. For 1 minute, you automatically know when your focused target is lying, your focused target can’t be invisible to you, and you can pinpoint its location while it remains within 60 feet of you.

options.label: Tiwaz (Level 2)
options.description:
Lesser Power: When you sketch tiwaz, if you hit your focused target with an attack, you can choose for the next attack made against it before the end of your next turn to have Advantage. If this subsequent attack hits, it deals extra damage equal to your level; then your rune ends.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch tiwaz in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you to become your focused target. For 1 minute, you automatically know when your focused target is lying, your focused target can’t be invisible to you, and you can pinpoint its location while it remains within 60 feet of you.

options.label: Tiwaz (Level 7)
options.description:
Greater Power: When you sketch tiwaz, you can’t be Frightened by your focused target. Additionally, the first time your focused target hits you with an attack, you have Resistance against the damage type caused by that attack until the end of your next turn. Then your rune ends.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch tiwaz in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you to become your focused target. For 1 minute, you automatically know when your focused target is lying, your focused target can’t be invisible to you, and you can pinpoint its location while it remains within 60 feet of you.

Lesser Power: When you sketch tiwaz, if you hit your focused target with an attack, you can choose for the next attack made against it before the end of your next turn to have Advantage. If this subsequent attack hits, it deals extra damage equal to your level; then your rune ends.

options.label: Tiwaz (Level 12)
options.description:
Patron Power: While you remain within 100 feet of Tyr, if your focused target is within 60 feet of you and deals damage to anyone other than you, you can use a Reaction to take half the damage upon yourself. If you attack the focused target on your next turn and successfully hit, you deal bonus Force damage equal to the amount of damage you took from your Reaction. Then your rune ends. You can sketch and use tiwaz this way as often as you want, provided you stay within 100 feet of Tyr.

Least Power: Once per day, you can sketch tiwaz in the air (no action required). Upon doing so, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you to become your focused target. For 1 minute, you automatically know when your focused target is lying, your focused target can’t be invisible to you, and you can pinpoint its location while it remains within 60 feet of you.

Lesser Power: When you sketch tiwaz, if you hit your focused target with an attack, you can choose for the next attack made against it before the end of your next turn to have Advantage. If this subsequent attack hits, it deals extra damage equal to your level; then your rune ends.

Greater Power: When you sketch tiwaz, you can’t be Frightened by your focused target. Additionally, the first time your focused target hits you with an attack, you have Resistance against the damage type caused by that attack until the end of your next turn. Then your rune ends.

options.label: Comprehend Languages
options.description:
For the duration, you understand the literal meaning of any spoken language that you hear. You also understand any written language that you see, but you must be touching the surface on which the words are written. It takes about 1 minute to read one page of text.

This spell doesn't decode secret messages in a text or a glyph, such as an arcane sigil, that isn't part of a written language.

options.label: Detect Evil and Good
options.description:
For the duration, you know if there is an aberration, celestial, elemental, fey, fiend, or undead within 30 feet of you, as well as where the creature is located. Similarly, you know if there is a place or object within 30 feet of you that has been magically consecrated or desecrated.

The spell can penetrate most barriers, but it is blocked by 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, a thin sheet of lead, or 3 feet of wood or dirt.

options.label: Detect Magic
options.description:
For the duration, you sense the presence of magic within 30 feet of you. If you sense magic in this way, you can use your action to see a faint aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears magic, and you learn its school of magic, if any.

The spell can penetrate most barriers, but it is blocked by 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, a thin sheet of lead, or 3 feet of wood or dirt.

options.label: Detect Poison and Disease
options.description:
For the duration, you can sense the presence and location of poisons, poisonous creatures, and diseases within 30 feet of you. You also identify the kind of poison, poisonous creature, or disease in each case.

The spell can penetrate most barriers, but it is blocked by 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, a thin sheet of lead, or 3 feet of wood or dirt.

options.label: Hunter's Mark
options.description:
You choose a creature you can see within range and mystically mark it as your quarry. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 damage to the target whenever you hit it with a weapon attack, and you have advantage on any Wisdom (Perception) or Wisdom (Survival) check you make to find it. If the target drops to 0 hit points before this spell ends, you can use a bonus action on a subsequent turn of yours to mark a new creature.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd or 4th level, you can maintain your concentration on the spell for up to 8 hours. When you use a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you can maintain your concentration on the spell for up to 24 hours.

options.label: Identify
options.description:
You choose one object that you must touch throughout the casting of the spell. If it is a magic item or some other magic-imbued object, you learn its properties and how to use them, whether it requires attunement to use, and how many charges it has, if any. You learn whether any spells are affecting the item and what they are. If the item was created by a spell, you learn which spell created it.

If you instead touch a creature throughout the casting, you learn what spells, if any, are currently affecting it.

options.label: Speak with Animals
options.description: You gain the ability to comprehend and verbally communicate with beasts for the duration. The knowledge and awareness of many beasts is limited by their intelligence, but at minimum, beasts can give you information about nearby locations and monsters, including whatever they can perceive or have perceived within the past day. You might be able to persuade a beast to perform a small favor for you, at the GM's discretion.

options.label: Beast Bond
options.description: You establish a telepathic link with one beast you touch that is friendly to you or charmed by you. The spell fails if the beast’s Intelligence is 4 or higher. Until the spell ends, the link is active while you and the beast are within line of sight of each other. Through the link, the beast can understand your telepathic messages to it, and it can telepathically communicate simple emotions and concepts back to you. While the link is active, the beast gains advantage on attack rolls against any creature within 5 feet of you that you can see.

options.label: Gift of Alacrity
options.description: You touch a willing creature. For the duration, the target can add 1d8 to its initiative rolls.

options.label: Comprehend Languages
options.description: For the duration, you understand the literal meaning of any language that you hear or see signed. You also understand any written language that you see, but you must be touching the surface on which the words are written. It takes about 1 minute to read one page of text. This spell doesn’t decode symbols or secret messages.

options.label: Speak with Animals
options.description:
For the duration, you can comprehend and verbally communicate with Beasts, and you can use any of the Influence action’s skill options with them.

Most Beasts have little to say about topics that don’t pertain to survival or companionship, but at minimum, a Beast can give you information about nearby locations and monsters, including whatever it has perceived within the past day.

options.label: Detect Evil and Good
options.description:
For the duration, you sense the location of any Aberration, Celestial, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, or Undead within 30 feet of yourself. You also sense whether the Hallow spell is active there and, if so, where.

The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

options.label: Detect Poison and Disease
options.description:
For the duration, you sense the location of poisons, poisonous or venomous creatures, and magical contagions within 30 feet of yourself. You sense the kind of poison, creature, or contagion in each case.

The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

options.label: Detect Magic
options.description:
For the duration, you sense the presence of magical effects within 30 feet of yourself. If you sense such effects, you can take the Magic action to see a faint aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears the magic, and if an effect was created by a spell, you learn the spell’s school of magic.

The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.

options.label: Hunter's Mark
options.description:
You magically mark one creature you can see within range as your quarry. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 Force damage to the target whenever you hit it with an attack roll. You also have Advantage on any Wisdom (Perception or Survival) check you make to find it.

If the target drops to 0 Hit Points before this spell ends, you can take a Bonus Action to move the mark to a new creature you can see within range.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Your Concentration can last longer with a spell slot of level 3–4 (up to 8 hours) or 5+ (up to 24 hours).

options.label: Identify
options.description:
You touch an object throughout the spell’s casting. If the object is a magic item or some other magical object, you learn its properties and how to use them, whether it requires Attunement, and how many charges it has, if any. You learn whether any ongoing spells are affecting the item and what they are. If the item was created by a spell, you learn that spell’s name.

If you instead touch a creature throughout the casting, you learn which ongoing spells, if any, are currently affecting it.

options.label: Animal Friendship
options.description:
This spell lets you convince a beast that you mean it no harm. Choose a beast that you can see within range. It must see and hear you. If the beast's Intelligence is 4 or higher, the spell fails. Otherwise, the beast must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for the spell's duration. If you or one of your companions harms the target, the spell ends.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can affect one additional beast for each slot level above 1st.

options.label: Bane
options.description:
Up to three creatures of your choice that you can see within range must make Charisma saving throws. Whenever a target that fails this saving throw makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, the target must roll a d4 and subtract the number rolled from the attack roll or saving throw.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st.

options.label: Bless
options.description:
You bless up to three creatures of your choice within range. Whenever a target makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, the target can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to the attack roll or saving throw.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st.

options.label: Charm Person
options.description:
You attempt to charm a humanoid you can see within range. It must make a Wisdom saving throw, and does so with advantage if you or your companions are fighting it. If it fails the saving throw, it is charmed by you until the spell ends or until you or your companions do anything harmful to it. The charmed creature regards you as a friendly acquaintance. When the spell ends, the creature knows it was charmed by you.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st. The creatures must be within 30 feet of each other when you target them.

options.label: Command
options.description:
You speak a one-word command to a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or follow the command on its next turn. The spell has no effect if the target is undead, if it doesn't understand your language, or if your command is directly harmful to it.

Some typical commands and their effects follow. You might issue a command other than one described here. If you do so, the DM determines how the target behaves. If the target can't follow your command, the spell ends.

Approach. The target moves toward you by the shortest and most direct route, ending its turn if it moves within 5 feet of you.

Drop. The target drops whatever it is holding and then ends its turn.

Flee. The target spends its turn moving away from you by the fastest available means.

Grovel. The target falls prone and then ends its turn.

Halt. The target doesn't move and takes no actions. A flying creature stays aloft, provided that it is able to do so. If it must move to stay aloft, it flies the minimum distance needed to remain in the air.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can affect one additional creature for each slot level above 1st. The creatures must be within 30 feet of each other when you target them.

options.label: Heroism
options.description:
A willing creature you touch is imbued with bravery. Until the spell ends, the creature is immune to being frightened and gains temporary hit points equal to your spellcasting ability modifier at the start of each of its turns. When the spell ends, the target loses any remaining temporary hit points from this spell.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st.

options.label: Hideous Laughter
options.description:
A creature of your choice that you can see within range perceives everything as hilariously funny and falls into fits of laughter if this spell affects it. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or fall prone, becoming incapacitated and unable to stand up for the duration. A creature with an Intelligence score of 4 or less isn't affected.

At the end of each of its turns, and each time it takes damage, the target can make another Wisdom saving throw. The target has advantage on the saving throw if it's triggered by damage. On a success, the spell ends.

options.label: Sleep
options.description:
This spell sends creatures into a magical slumber. Roll 5d8; the total is how many hit points of creatures this spell can affect. Creatures within 20 feet of a point you choose within range are affected in ascending order of their current hit points (ignoring unconscious creatures).

Starting with the creature that has the lowest current hit points, each creature affected by this spell falls unconscious until the spell ends, the sleeper takes damage, or someone uses an action to shake or slap the sleeper awake. Subtract each creature’s hit points from the total before moving on to the creature with the next lowest hit points. A creature’s hit points must be equal to or less than the remaining total for that creature to be affected.

Undead and creatures immune to being charmed aren’t affected by this spell.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, roll an additional 2d8 for each slot level above 1st.

options.label: Compelled Duel
options.description:
You attempt to compel a creature into a duel. One creature that you can see within range must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is drawn to you, compelled by your divine demand. For the duration, it has disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures other than you, and must make a Wisdom saving throw each time it attempts to move to a space that is more than 30 feet away from you; if it succeeds on this saving throw, this spell doesn’t restrict the target’s movement for that turn.

The spell ends if you attack any other creature, if you cast a spell that targets a hostile creature other than the target, if a creature friendly to you damages the target or casts a harmful spell on it, or if you end your turn more than 30 feet away from the target.

options.label: Dissonant Whispers
options.description:
You whisper a discordant melody that only one creature of your choice within range can hear, wracking it with terrible pain. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 3d6 psychic damage and must immediately use its reaction, if available, to move as far as its speed allows away from you. The creature doesn’t move into obviously dangerous ground, such as a fire or a pit. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage and doesn’t have to move away. A deafened creature automatically succeeds on the save.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 1st.

options.label: Hex
options.description:
You place a curse on a creature that you can see within range. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 necrotic damage to the target whenever you hit it with an attack. Also, choose one ability when you cast the spell. The target has disadvantage on ability checks made with the chosen ability.

If the target drops to 0 hit points before this spell ends, you can use a bonus action on a subsequent turn of yours to curse a new creature.

A remove curse cast on the target ends this spell early.

At Higher Levels. When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd or 4th level, you can maintain your concentration on the spell for up to 8 hours. When you use a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you can maintain your concentration on the spell for up to 24 hours.

options.label: Tasha's Hideous Laughter
options.description:
A creature of your choice that you can see within range perceives everything as hilariously funny and falls into fits of laughter if this spell affects it. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or fall prone, becoming incapacitated and unable to stand up for the duration. A creature with an Intelligence score of 4 or less isn’t affected.

At the end of each of its turns, and each time it takes damage, the target can make another Wisdom saving throw. The target has advantage on the saving throw if it’s triggered by damage. On a success, the spell ends.

options.label: Silvery Barbs
options.description:
You magically distract the triggering creature and turn its momentary uncertainty into encouragement for another creature. The triggering creature must reroll the d20 and use the lower roll.

You can then choose a different creature you can see within range (you can choose yourself). The chosen creature has advantage on the next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw it makes within 1 minute. A creature can be empowered by only one use of this spell at a time.

options.label: Animal Friendship
options.description:
Target a Beast that you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Charmed condition for the duration. If you or one of your allies deals damage to the target, the spell ends.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional Beast for each spell slot level above 1.

options.label: Bane
options.description:
Up to three creatures of your choice that you can see within range must each make a Charisma saving throw. Whenever a target that fails this save makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, the target must subtract 1d4 from the attack roll or save.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

options.label: Bless
options.description:
You bless up to three creatures within range. Whenever a target makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, the target adds 1d4 to the attack roll or save.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

options.label: Charm Person
options.description:
One Humanoid you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. It does so with Advantage if you or your allies are fighting it. On a failed save, the target has the Charmed condition until the spell ends or until you or your allies damage it. The Charmed creature is Friendly to you. When the spell ends, the target knows it was Charmed by you.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

options.label: Command
options.description:
You speak a one-word command to a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or follow the command on its next turn. Choose the command from these options:

Approach. The target moves toward you by the shortest and most direct route, ending its turn if it moves within 5 feet of you.

Drop. The target drops whatever it is holding and then ends its turn.

Flee. The target spends its turn moving away from you by the fastest available means.

Grovel. The target has the Prone condition and then ends its turn.

Halt. On its turn, the target doesn’t move and takes no action or Bonus Action.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can affect one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

options.label: Hex
options.description:
You place a curse on a creature that you can see within range. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 Necrotic damage to the target whenever you hit it with an attack roll. Also, choose one ability when you cast the spell. The target has Disadvantage on ability checks made with the chosen ability.

If the target drops to 0 Hit Points before this spell ends, you can take a Bonus Action on a later turn to curse a new creature.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. Your Concentration can last longer with a spell slot of level 2 (up to 4 hours), 3–4 (up to 8 hours), or 5+ (24 hours).

options.label: Compelled Duel
options.description:
You try to compel a creature into a duel. One creature that you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target has Disadvantage on attack rolls against creatures other than you, and it can’t willingly move to a space that is more than 30 feet away from you.

The spell ends if you make an attack roll against a creature other than the target, if you cast a spell on an enemy other than the target, if an ally of yours damages the target, or if you end your turn more than 30 feet away from the target.

options.label: Sleep
options.description:
Each creature of your choice in a 5-foot-radius Sphere centered on a point within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the Incapacitated condition until the end of its next turn, at which point it must repeat the save. If the target fails the second save, the target has the Unconscious condition for the duration. The spell ends on a target if it takes damage or someone within 5 feet of it takes an action to shake it out of the spell’s effect.

Creatures that don’t sleep, such as elves, or that have Immunity to the Exhaustion condition automatically succeed on saves against this spell.

options.label: Dissonant Whispers
options.description:
One creature of your choice that you can see within range hears a discordant melody in its mind. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 3d6 Psychic damage and must immediately use its Reaction, if available, to move as far away from you as it can, using the safest route. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d6 for each spell slot level above 1.

options.label: Tasha's Hideous Laughter
options.description:
One creature of your choice that you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it has the Prone and Incapacitated conditions for the duration. During that time, it laughs uncontrollably if it’s capable of laughter, and it can’t end the Prone condition on itself.

At the end of each of its turns and each time it takes damage, it makes another Wisdom saving throw. The target has Advantage on the save if the save is triggered by damage. On a successful save, the spell ends.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

options.label: Heroism
options.description:
A willing creature you touch is imbued with bravery. Until the spell ends, the creature is immune to the Frightened condition and gains Temporary Hit Points equal to your spellcasting ability modifier at the start of each of its turns.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

options.label: Insidious Rhythm
options.description:
One creature of your choice that you can see within range hears a haunting yet catchy tune that continuously repeats in its mind. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or it has Disadvantage on Intelligence checks and on Constitution saving throws made to maintain Concentration. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.

options.label: Heavenly Wings
options.description:
Once on each of your turns before the transformation ends, you can deal extra damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or a spell. The extra damage equals your Proficiency Bonus, and the extra damage’s type is Radiant.

Heavenly Wings. Two spectral wings sprout from your back temporarily. Until the transformation ends, you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed.

options.label: Inner Radiance
options.description:
Once on each of your turns before the transformation ends, you can deal extra damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or a spell. The extra damage equals your Proficiency Bonus, and the extra damage’s type is Radiant.

Inner Radiance. Searing light temporarily radiates from your eyes and mouth. For the duration, you shed Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet, and at the end of each of your turns, each creature within 10 feet of you takes Radiant damage equal to your Proficiency Bonus.

options.label: Necrotic Shroud
options.description:
Once on each of your turns before the transformation ends, you can deal extra damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or a spell. The extra damage equals your Proficiency Bonus, and the extra damage’s type is Necrotic.

Necrotic Shroud. Your eyes briefly become pools of darkness, and flightless wings sprout from your back temporarily. Creatures other than your allies within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Charisma saving throw (DC 8 plus your Charisma modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or have the Frightened condition until the end of your next turn.

options.label: Increase three scores (+1 / +1 / +1)
options.description: Increase three different scores by 1.

options.label: Increase two scores (+2 / +1)
options.description: Increase one scores by 2 and a different score by 1.

race.name: Healing Hands
race.description: As a Magic action, you touch a creature and roll a number of d4s equal to your Proficiency Bonus. The creature regains a number of Hit Points equal to the total rolled. Once you use this trait, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.
race.snippet: Once per Long Rest as a Magic action, you touch a creature and they regain {{proficiency#unsigned}}d4 HP.

race.name: Celestial Revelation
race.description:
When you reach character level 3, you can transform as a Bonus Action using one of the options below (choose the option each time you transform). The transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you end it (no action required). Once you transform, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

Once on each of your turns before the transformation ends, you can deal extra damage to one target when you deal damage to it with an attack or a spell. The extra damage equals your Proficiency Bonus, and the extra damage’s type is either Necrotic for Necrotic Shroud or Radiant for Heavenly Wings and Inner Radiance.

Here are the transformation options:

Heavenly Wings. Two spectral wings sprout from your back temporarily. Until the transformation ends, you have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed.

Inner Radiance. Searing light temporarily radiates from your eyes and mouth. For the duration, you shed Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet, and at the end of each of your turns, each creature within 10 feet of you takes Radiant damage equal to your Proficiency Bonus.

Necrotic Shroud. Your eyes briefly become pools of darkness, and flightless wings sprout from your back temporarily. Creatures other than your allies within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Charisma saving throw (DC 8 plus your Charisma modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or have the [condition]Frightened[/condition] condition until the end of your next turn.
race.snippet: Once per Long Rest, you transform using one of the options available to you (choose the option each time you transform). The transformation lasts for 1 minute or until you end it (no action required).

class.name: Sneak Attack
class.description:
You know how to strike subtly and exploit a foe’s distraction. Once per turn, you can deal an extra 1d6 damage to one creature you hit with an attack roll if you have Advantage on the roll and the attack uses a [wprop]Finesse[/wprop] or a Ranged weapon. The extra damage’s type is the same as the weapon’s type.

You don’t need Advantage on the attack roll if at least one of your allies is within 5 feet of the target, the ally doesn’t have the [condition]Incapacitated[/condition] condition, and you don’t have Disadvantage on the attack roll.

The extra damage increases as you gain Rogue levels, as shown in the Sneak Attack column of the Rogue Features table.
class.snippet: Once per turn you can deal an extra {{(classlevel/2)@roundup}}d6 damage to one creature you hit with an attack if you have Advantage on the roll and the attack uses a Finesse or Ranged weapon. The extra damage’s type is the same as the weapon’s type. You don’t need Advantage on the attack if at least one of your allies is within 5 ft. of the target, the ally doesn’t have the Incapacitated condition, and you don’t have Disadvantage on the attack roll.

class.name: Cunning Action
class.description: Your quick thinking and agility allow you to move and act quickly. On your turn, you can take one of the following actions as a Bonus Action: [action]Dash[/action], [action]Disengage[/action], or [action]Hide[/action].
class.snippet: On your turn, you can take one of the following actions as a Bonus Action: Dash, Disengage, or Hide.

class.name: Steady Aim
class.description: As a Bonus Action, you give yourself Advantage on your next attack roll on the current turn. You can use this feature only if you haven’t moved during this turn, and after you use it, your Speed is 0 until the end of the current turn.
class.snippet: You give yourself Advantage on your next attack roll on the current turn, provided that you haven’t moved during this turn. After you use it, your Speed is 0 until the end of the current turn.

class.name: Sneak Attack: Poison (Cost: 1d6)
class.description:
You’ve developed cunning ways to use your Sneak Attack. When you deal Sneak Attack damage, you can add one of the following Cunning Strike effects. Each effect has a die cost, which is the number of Sneak Attack damage dice you must forgo to add the effect. You remove the die before rolling, and the effect occurs immediately after the attack’s damage is dealt. For example, if you add the Poison effect, remove 1d6 from the Sneak Attack’s damage before rolling.

If a Cunning Strike effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 plus your Dexterity modifier and Proficiency Bonus.

Poison (Cost: 1d6). You add a toxin to your strike, forcing the target to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the [condition]Poisoned[/condition] condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the [condition]Poisoned[/condition] target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success.

To use this effect, you must have a [items]Poisoner’s Kit[/items] on your person.

Trip (Cost: 1d6). If the target is Large or smaller, it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the [condition]Prone[/condition] condition.

Withdraw (Cost: 1d6). Immediately after the attack, you move up to half your Speed without provoking [action]Opportunity Attack;Opportunity Attacks[/action].
class.snippet: You add a toxin to your strike, forcing the target to make a DC {{savedc:dex}} Con. saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the Poisoned condition for 1 minute, and at the end of each of its turns, the Poisoned target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. You must have a Poisoner’s Kit on your person to use this effect.

class.saveFailDescription: If the target is Large or smaller, it has the Prone condition.

class.snippet: Immediately after the attack, you move up to half your Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

class.name: Uncanny Dodge
class.description: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to halve the attack’s damage against you (round down).
class.snippet: When an attacker you can see hits you with an attack, you can take a Reaction to halve the attack’s damage against you (round down).

class.name: Initiate a Circle Spell
class.description: You take a Magic action to initiate casting a Circle spell. When you do so, choose which Circle casting option you’re using for this casting; you must also meet any of the other requirements described in that option’s text. Until the Circle spell’s casting is complete, you must maintain Concentration on the spell.
class.snippet: You take a Magic action to initiate casting a Circle spell. When you do so, choose which Circle casting option you’re using for this casting; you must also meet any of the other requirements described in that option’s text. Until the Circle spell’s casting is complete, you must maintain Concentration on the spell.

class.name: Circle Spell: Augment
class.description: When you cast a spell with a range of at least 5 feet, you can increase the range of the spell by 1,000 feet per secondary caster contributing to the spell, up to a maximum of a 1-mile increase.
class.snippet: When you cast a spell with a range of at least 5 ft., you can increase the range of the spell by 1,000 ft. per secondary caster contributing to the spell, up to a max of a 1-mile increase.

class.name: Circle Spell: Distribute
class.description: When you cast a spell that requires Concentration, you can distribute the mental load of the spell among you and the secondary casters. Once the casting is complete, you and all secondary casters can maintain Concentration on this spell. As long as at least one caster who contributed to the spell maintains this Concentration, the spell’s effects remain active.
class.snippet: When you cast a spell that requires Concentration, you can distribute the mental load of the spell among you and the secondary casters. Once the casting is complete, you and all secondary casters can maintain Concentration on this spell. As long as at least one caster who contributed to the spell maintains this Concentration, the spell’s effects remain active.

class.name: Circle Spell: Expand
class.description:
When you cast a spell that creates an area of effect, you can increase one dimension of the spell’s area of effect for this casting by 10 feet per secondary caster contributing to the spell. For example, if a spell creates a 20-foot-radius Sphere, casting it as a Circle spell with two secondary casters would increase the radius to 40 feet. A spell that creates a Line that is 5 feet wide and 300 feet long, cast as a Circle spell with two secondary casters, could make a Line that is either 25 feet wide or 320 feet long.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.
class.snippet:
When you cast a spell that creates an area of effect, you can increase one dimension of the spell’s area of effect for this casting by 10 ft. per secondary caster contributing to the spell. 

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

class.name: Circle Spell: Prolong
class.description:
When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can increase the duration of the spell depending on the number of secondary casters contributing to the spell, as detailed in the table below.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

Spell Duration
Secondary Casters The Spell Increases Duration by
1–3 1 hour
4–6 8 hours
7+ 24 hours
class.snippet:
When you cast a spell that has a duration of 1 min or longer, you can increase the duration of the spell depending on the number of secondary casters contributing to the spell.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

class.name: Circle Spell: Safeguard
class.description:
When you cast a spell that creates an area of effect, you can carve out a safe zone within that area of effect that is unaffected by the spell for its duration. This safe zone consists of a number of 5-foot Cubes equal to your spellcasting ability modifier plus the number of secondary casters contributing to the spell (minimum of one Cube). You can arrange the Cubes as you like, but each Cube must be contiguous with at least one other Cube.

If the spell’s area of effect can be moved, the safe zone moves with it.
class.snippet: When you cast a spell that creates an area of effect, you can carve out a safe zone within that area of effect that is unaffected by the spell for its duration. This safe zone consists of a number of 5-ft. Cubes equal to {{modifier:cha#unsigned}} + the number of secondary casters contributing to the spell (min of 1 Cube). You can arrange the Cubes as you like, but each Cube must be contiguous with at least one other Cube. If the spell’s area of effect can be moved, the safe zone moves with it.

class.name: Circle Spell: Supplant
class.description:
When you cast a spell that requires at least one Material component with a specified cost that is consumed by the spell, you can reduce the minimum cost of one such Material component by 50 GP per each secondary caster contributing to the spell. For example, casting [spells]Revivify[/spells] as a Circle spell with two secondary casters would reduce the Material component’s minimum cost to 200 GP.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot of a level greater than or equal to the spell’s level (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.
class.snippet:
When you cast a spell that requires at least one Material component with a specified cost that is consumed by the spell, you can reduce the min cost of one such Material component by 50 GP per each secondary caster contributing to the spell.

Each secondary caster contributing to the spell must expend a spell slot of a level greater than or equal to the spell’s level (no action required). If the spell fails, these spell slots aren’t expended.

feat.name: Piercer: Puncture
feat.description: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals Piercing damage, you can reroll one of the attack’s damage dice, and you must use the new roll.
feat.snippet: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals Piercing damage, you can reroll one of the attack’s damage dice, and you must use the new roll.

feat.name: Piercer: Enhanced Critical
feat.description: When you score a Critical Hit that deals Piercing damage to a creature, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the extra Piercing damage the target takes.
feat.snippet: When you score a Critical Hit that deals Piercing damage to a creature, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the extra Piercing damage the target takes.

feat.name: Nick (Dagger)
feat.description:
Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery property of Daggers:

Nick. When you make the extra attack of the [wprop]Light[/wprop] property, you can make it as part of the [action]Attack[/action] action instead of as a Bonus Action. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.
feat.snippet: Nick. When you make the extra attack of the Light property, you can make it as part of the Attack action instead of as a Bonus Action. This extra attack can only be made once per turn.

feat.name: Vex (Shortbow)
feat.description:
Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery property of Shortbows:

Vex. If you hit a creature with a Shortbow and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.
feat.snippet: Vex. If you hit a creature with a Shortbow and deal damage to it, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

definition.name: Acid Splash
definition.description:
You create an acidic bubble at a point within range, where it explodes in a 5-foot-radius Sphere. Each creature in that Sphere must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take 1d6 Acid damage.

Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d6 when you reach levels 5 (2d6), 11 (3d6), and 17 (4d6).

definition.name: Fire Bolt
definition.description:
You hurl a mote of fire at a creature or an object within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 Fire damage. A flammable object hit by this spell starts burning if it isn’t being worn or carried.

Cantrip Upgrade. The damage increases by 1d10 when you reach levels 5 (2d10), 11 (3d10), and 17 (4d10).

definition.name: Expeditious Retreat
definition.description: You take the Dash action, and until the spell ends, you can take that action again as a Bonus Action.

definition.name: Grease
definition.description:
Nonflammable grease covers the ground in a 10-foot square centered on a point within range and turns it into Difficult Terrain for the duration.

When the grease appears, each creature standing in its area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the Prone condition. A creature that enters the area or ends its turn there must also succeed on that save or fall Prone.
definition.componentsDescription: a bit of pork rind or butter

definition.name: Identify
definition.description:
You touch an object throughout the spell’s casting. If the object is a magic item or some other magical object, you learn its properties and how to use them, whether it requires Attunement, and how many charges it has, if any. You learn whether any ongoing spells are affecting the item and what they are. If the item was created by a spell, you learn that spell’s name.

If you instead touch a creature throughout the casting, you learn which ongoing spells, if any, are currently affecting it.

definition.name: Silent Image
definition.description:
You create the image of an object, a creature, or some other visible phenomenon that is no larger than a 15-foot Cube. The image appears at a spot within range and lasts for the duration. The image is purely visual; it isn’t accompanied by sound, smell, or other sensory effects.

As a Magic action, you can cause the image to move to any spot within range. As the image changes location, you can alter its appearance so that its movements appear natural for the image. For example, if you create an image of a creature and move it, you can alter the image so that it appears to be walking.

Physical interaction with the image reveals it to be an illusion, since things can pass through it. A creature that takes a Study action to examine the image can determine that it is an illusion with a successful Intelligence (Investigation) check against your spell save DC. If a creature discerns the illusion for what it is, the creature can see through the image.

definition.name: Spellfire Flare
definition.description:
You unleash a blast of brilliant fire. Make a ranged spell attack against a target within range; a target gains no benefit from Half Cover or Three-Quarters Cover for this attack roll. On a hit, the target takes 2d10 Radiant damage.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. You create an additional blast for each spell slot level above 1. You can direct the blasts at the same target or at different ones. Make a separate attack roll for each blast.

modifiers.restriction: Target gains no benefit from Half or Three-Quarters Cover. You can direct the blasts at the same target or at different ones (2d10 each).

definition.name: Thunderwave
definition.description:
You unleash a wave of thunderous energy. Each creature in a 15-foot Cube originating from you makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d8 Thunder damage and is pushed 10 feet away from you. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.

In addition, unsecured objects that are entirely within the Cube are pushed 10 feet away from you, and a thunderous boom is audible within 300 feet.

Using a Higher-Level Spell Slot. The damage increases by 1d8 for each spell slot level above 1.
